user_query:
I need a pragmatic 7-day execution plan for my product team that aligns with our Q2 OKRs and the latest stakeholder notes. Please read these files from input/:
- input/okr.yaml (Q2 OKRs with keys like OBJx/KRy)
- input/meeting_notes.md (stakeholder constraints, decisions, and dependencies)
- input/backlog.csv (columns: id,title,effort,urgency,owner,dependencies)
- input/calendar.tsv (existing events, one per line with date, start, end, title)

Deliverables:
1) Write a machine-readable plan to output/weekly_plan.json using this exact schema:
{
  "top_priorities": [
    {
      "id": "<backlog id>",
      "title": "<short title>",
      "rationale": "<2-3 sentence why now, referencing constraints and trade-offs>",
      "aligned_okrs": ["<OKR key>", "<OKR key>"]
    },
    { ... },
    { ... }
  ],
  "schedule": [
    {
      "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "blocks": [
        {"start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM", "task_id": "<backlog id>", "label": "<optional label>"},
        {"start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM", "label": "Buffer"}
      ]
    },
    "... exactly 7 unique days total ..."
  ],
  "risks": [
    {"description": "<risk>", "impact": "low|medium|high", "mitigation": "<concrete mitigation>"},
    {"description": "<risk>", "impact": "low|medium|high", "mitigation": "<concrete mitigation>"},
    {"description": "<risk>", "impact": "low|medium|high", "mitigation": "<concrete mitigation>"}
  ],
  "assumptions": ["<explicit assumption>", "<explicit assumption>"]
}
Requirements for weekly_plan.json:
- top_priorities must contain exactly 3 items, each referencing real backlog ids and at least one OKR key in aligned_okrs.
- schedule must include exactly 7 unique dates, each with at least 2 blocks. Times must be HH:MM 24-hour format with start < end and no overlaps within a day.
- Include at least 3 risks with clear mitigations and calibrated impact levels.
- Include at least 2 explicit assumptions.

2) Write a human-readable brief to output/summary.md with the following sections and content:
- "Top 3 Priorities" (name each priority and briefly explain why now)
- "Risks & Mitigations" (bullet list; match the JSON risks)
- "Assumptions" (explicit list)
- "Verification checklist" (at least 5 items in the form "- [ ] ..." for me to validate quickly)
- "Fallbacks" (at least 2 concrete fallback strategies if a key assumption fails)

Please ensure the priority titles used in summary.md exactly match those in weekly_plan.json, avoid scheduling overlaps, and state any uncertainties plainly. Keep the writing concise and execution-focused.
